hypotheses.root_cause: A reconnect callback dereferences a null or stale context pointer after Wi-Fi reconnect.
evidence_for: The failure appears after Wi-Fi reconnect
Disabling the reconnect callback is the last known good change
evidence_against: No ELF is available, so the PC/backtrace is not symbolicated
Heap poisoning output is not included
hypotheses.verification_step: Rebuild with matching ELF, run esp_panic_parse.py, symbolicate the backtrace, and add a guard plus lifetime check around the reconnect context.
hypotheses.expected_observation: If this branch is correct, symbolication lands inside the reconnect callback and the guard prevents the LoadProhibited panic.
hypotheses.fix_if_confirmed: Fix context ownership or null handling in the reconnect callback and add a panic-log regression packet with the matching ELF.

hypotheses.root_cause: Heap corruption damages the callback context before reconnect.
evidence_for: Use-after-free can present as a stale pointer panic
evidence_against: No heap poisoning assertion appears in the sanitized log
hypotheses.verification_step: Enable heap poisoning and allocation tracing around reconnect.
hypotheses.expected_observation: A heap issue would produce a poisoning violation or allocation lifetime mismatch before the panic.
hypotheses.fix_if_confirmed: Fix ownership and preserve heap trace evidence.
